text: This paper reviews briefly the role of rare earth elements (REEs) in the world economy, particularly with respect to the permanent magnets that consume mainly the Nd–Pr–Dy mixtures and smaller amounts of Ce and Gd. The magnet sector is the largest volume rare earth magnet (REM) sector and is growing faster than the other sectors. The analysis is based on a report “Rare Earths, Outlook to 2030” released on 3 February 2021 in Green Car Congress, a business-to-business online publication, and obtained in June 2021 by Roskill, which was acquired in April 2021 by the leading global research, consultancy, and data analytics business to the natural resources sector, Wood Mackenzie. The report has provided a unique overview of supply of rare earths to all four markets—US, Europe, Japan and China.
headline: Fully Human Written
